title: Distephanus glutinosus
taxonomy: Distephanus glutinosus is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Distephanus and is a member of the tribe Heliantheae. The plant is native to tropical regions and is often found in dry and sandy areas. Distephanus glutinosus is also known as sticky bush or sticky flower. The species is native to southern Africa.
uses: Distephanus glutinosus is traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating skin conditions. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may contribute to its therapeutic effects. In some parts of the world, the plant is used to treat wounds, burns, and other skin irritations. The plant's sticky sap, known as latex, is often collected and used to create topical treatments for various skin conditions. However, more research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal properties and potential applications.

intro: Distephanus glutinosus is a species of flowering plant in the sunflower family (Asteraceae), native to tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
